summary: First full E2E test run for Order-Payment Reconciliation Dashboard. Backend: 14/14 pytest tests passed covering auth (signup/login/me), CSV upload+reconcile, KPIs, discrepancy filtering, LLM explain caching, CSV validation, and user data isolation. Frontend: Playwright flow succeeded end-to-end - signup, dashboard empty state, CSV upload via hidden inputs, reconcile with correct KPI values (10 orders, 9 payments, 2 reconciled, 9 discrepancies, $865.10 at risk), Recharts chart, filterable discrepancy table, Explain sheet with LLM summary/likely_cause/suggested_action, regenerate, runs history navigation, and logout.

issues: Recharts logs warning: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0'. Chart still renders correctly after data loads but the console warning appears during initial paint. Consider setting explicit minWidth/minHeight or aspect on ResponsiveContainer.

action_items: (Non-blocking) Silence Recharts ResponsiveContainer width/height=-1 warning by giving the chart container a defined min height or explicit dimensions on initial render.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:203-206 - _validate_upload is called AFTER await file.read() which means large files (>5MB) are still fully read into memory before the size check rejects. Consider streaming/checking content-length header or reading in chunks with early abort to avoid memory pressure on abuse. Tests still pass since 6MB is small.
server.py:243 - db.orders.insert_many uses the same normalized dicts that are also referenced by reconcile(); insert_many mutates docs by adding Mongo _id. Currently dict(o) is copied so safe, good.

reconcile.py:283-298 - ORPHAN_PAYMENT iterates ALL payments including those already matched (payments whose order_id IS in orders but the payment was e.g. failed will still not be orphan since order_id is in order_ids). Rule is correct but comment could clarify.
llm.py:14 - MODEL_NAME='gpt-4.1-mini' but module docstring still says 'OpenAI gpt-5.2-mini'. Minor docstring drift.
auth.py:11 - JWT_SECRET is fetched via os.environ[...] - fails fast if missing which is good.
test_credentials: Fresh signups per run (tester_<ts>@example.com / testpass123). Existing account: e2e@test.com / testpass123.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests create fresh users each run via /api/auth/signup and upload /app/sample_data/orders.csv + payments.csv.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all endpoints incl. LLM explain caching (real gpt-4.1-mini call ~3-6s). Sample CSV totals: 10 orders, 9 payments, 9 discrepancies (all 9 types + MATCHED), total_money_at_risk_usd ~= 865.10. Frontend flow: on /auth click [data-testid=tab-signup] BEFORE submit; upload via 'label[data-testid=drop-orders] input[type=file]'. Reconciliation is synchronous <2s.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues. Only one console warning from Recharts on very first paint due to ResponsiveContainer receiving width/height of -1 before layout settles. Not user-visible; chart renders correctly.
